heading: Notice requirements
label: 300bb–6 Notice requirements
notes: References in TextThe Social Security Act, referred to in par. (3), is , . Titles II and XVI of the Social Security Act are classified generally to subchapters II (§ 401 et seq.) and XVI (§ 1381 et seq.), respectively, of chapter 7 of this title. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see and Tables.act Aug. 14, 1935, ch. 53149 Stat. 620section 1305 of this title

Amendments1996—Par. (3). substituted “at any time during the first 60 days of continuation coverage under this subchapter” for “at the time of a qualifying event described in ”.Pub. L. 104–191section 300bb–3(2) of this title1989—Par. (3). inserted “and each qualified beneficiary who is determined, under title II or XVI of the Social Security Act, to have been disabled at the time of a qualifying event described in is responsible for notifying the plan administrator of such determination within 60 days after the date of the determination and for notifying the plan administrator within 30 days after the date of any final determination under such title or titles that the qualified beneficiary is no longer disabled” after “date of the qualifying event”.Pub. (3). , as amended by , inserted “within 60 days after the date of the qualifying event”.Pub. L. 99–514Pub. L. 100–203

Notification to Covered Employees, , , provided that:Pub. L. 99–272, title X, § 10003(c)100 Stat. 236Apr. 7, 1986“At the time that the amendments made by this section [enacting this subchapter] apply to a group health plan (covered under section 2201 of the Public Health Service Act []), the plan shall notify each covered employee, and spouse of the employee (if any), who is covered under the plan at that time of the continuation coverage required under title XXII of such Act [ et seq.]. The notice furnished under this subsection is in lieu of notice that may otherwise be required under section 2206(1) of such Act [] with respect to such individuals.”42 U.S.C. 300bb–142 U.S.C. 300bb–142 U.S.C. 300bb–6(1)

children.text: the group health plan shall provide, at the time of commencement of coverage under the plan, written notice to each covered employee and spouse of the employee (if any) of the rights provided under this subsection,1So in original. Probably should be “subchapter”.1

the employer of an employee under a plan must notify the plan administrator of a qualifying event described in paragraph (1), (2), or (4) of within 30 days of the date of the qualifying event,section 300bb–3 of this title

each covered employee or qualified beneficiary is responsible for notifying the plan administrator of the occurrence of any qualifying event described in paragraph (3) or (5) of within 60 days after the date of the qualifying event and each qualified beneficiary who is determined, under title II or XVI of the Social Security Act [ et seq., 1381 et seq.], to have been disabled at any time during the first 60 days of continuation coverage under this subchapter is responsible for notifying the plan administrator of such determination within 60 days after the date of the determination and for notifying the plan administrator within 30 days after the date of any final determination under such title or titles that the qualified beneficiary is no longer disabled, andsection 300bb–3 of this title42 U.S.C. 401

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a qualifying event described in paragraph (1), (2), or (4) of , any qualified beneficiary with respect to such event, andsection 300bb–3 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a qualifying event described in paragraph (3) or (5) of where the covered employee notifies the plan administrator under paragraph (3), any qualified beneficiary with respect to such event,section 300bb–3 of this title
